cards.back: The concept of exposing only necessary information to the outside world while hiding internal details.
The process of bundling data and methods that operate on that data within a single unit, called a class or object.
The mechanism by which one class can inherit properties and behavior from another class.
The ability of an object or method to take on multiple forms, depending on the context in which it is used.
Self-contained units of data and code that represent a real-world entity, such as a person, a car, or a bank account.
